title: ajuga macrosperma var. thomsonii
intro: Ajuga macrosperma var. thomsonii, also known as Indian bugle or blue bugle, is a plant species that belongs to the genus Ajuga and is native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Ajuga macrosperma var. thomsonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
